varieties.name: RRII 105
varieties.traits: Very vigorous growth, thick bark (7-8mm), excellent wind resistance, fast girth increment
varieties.yield_notes: 1800-2200 kg/ha/year, widely considered the highest yielding Indian clone
varieties.growing_notes: Moderate resistance to abnormal leaf fall (Phytophthora), good resistance to powdery mildew

varieties.name: RRII 430
varieties.traits: High latex vessel density, thick virgin bark, strong wind resistance, vigorous growth
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically recommended for South Tamil Nadu and tropical monsoon climates regions by Indian government Good resistance to abnormal leaf fall, tolerates Phytophthora
varieties.availability: Available at tropical monsoon climates rubber nurseries including Thulumpanmakel (RR 11-430), recommended by Rubber Board

varieties.name: RRII 429
varieties.traits: Early maturity (can tap at 5-6 years), rapid growth, good bark thickness
varieties.yield_notes: 1800-2100 kg/ha/year, early tree opening for tapping
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for tropical monsoon climates, South Tamil Nadu, and Northeast India by Rubber Board Good overall disease tolerance
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, being promoted by Rubber Board for Northeast expansion

varieties.name: RRII 422
varieties.traits: Stable production, good bark consumption rate, moderate growth vigor
varieties.growing_notes: Good general disease tolerance

varieties.name: RRII 417
varieties.traits: Excellent dry rubber content in latex, good bark thickness, moderate vigor
varieties.growing_notes: Good disease tolerance

varieties.name: RRII 414
varieties.traits: Good growth, moderate bark thickness, stable production
varieties.growing_notes: Moderate disease resistance

varieties.name: RRII 203
varieties.traits: Dual-purpose: latex and timber, thick bark, vigorous growth, good bole development
varieties.yield_notes: 1500-1800 kg/ha/year latex plus good timber quality
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for Bangladesh and India. Excellent for combined latex and rubberwood production Good disease tolerance
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, recommended for latex-timber plantations

varieties.name: RRII 208
varieties.traits: Latex-timber dual purpose, good timber volume, moderate latex yield
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India, good for furniture timber production Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and South Indian nurseries

varieties.name: RRII 5
varieties.traits: Stable growth, moderate vigor, good adaptability
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India, one of the earlier RRII releases Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu nurseries

varieties.name: RRISL 203
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka, Rubber Research Institute of Sri Lanka, 2000s
varieties.traits: Good growth vigor, moderate wind resistance
varieties.growing_notes: Sri Lankan clone suitable for Tropical monsoon similar climate Good overall disease tolerance
varieties.availability: Can be imported to India, available through international clone exchange

varieties.name: RRIM 600
varieties.traits: Moderate growth, good bark thickness, stable production, responds well to tapping stimulation
varieties.growing_notes: Susceptible to Phytophthora leaf fall, moderate powdery mildew resistance

varieties.name: RRIM 703
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, good wind resistance, thick bark
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India, good secondary choice to RRIM 600 Good overall disease tolerance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Indian nurseries

varieties.name: RRIM 928-931, 2001-2020
varieties.traits: Fast-growing, long straight boles, dual-purpose latex-timber production, incorporate wider Amazonian genetic base
varieties.yield_notes: 1500-2000 kg/ha/year latex plus superior timber quality
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for Bangladesh. Modern latex-timber clones with broader genetic base. Good for rubberwood furniture industry. Variable, some SALB resistance from occidental parents
varieties.availability: Limited availability in India, may require import authorization

varieties.name: PB 86
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, good rooting characteristics, drought tolerance
varieties.growing_notes: Historic primary clone, commonly used as rootstock. Still recommended for cultivation in India. Important parent in many breeding programs. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu nurseries

varieties.name: PB 235
varieties.traits: Moderate growth, good bark thickness, stable production
varieties.growing_notes: Very popular in tropical monsoon climates and India. Recommended for multiclone plantings. Should not exceed 50% of total area. Susceptible to powdery mildew, moderate overall resistance
varieties.availability: Widely available - Thulumpanmakel and tropical monsoon climates nurseries stock PB 235

varieties.name: PB 260
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, high yield potential, good bark thickness
varieties.growing_notes: Popular Malaysian clone in tropical monsoon climates. Recommended for 5-10% of plantation area for evaluation. Use modified tapping systems to avoid TPD. Can be susceptible to tapping panel dryness (TPD) under intensive tapping
varieties.availability: Widely available - Thulumpanmakel (PB-260) and tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: PB 217
varieties.traits: Good growth, moderate vigor, stable production
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India, reliable performer Good overall tolerance
varieties.availability: Available at Thulumpanmakel (PB 217) and tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: PB 28/59
varieties.traits: Good growth, moderate vigor
varieties.growing_notes: Historically successful clone, still recommended for South India Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: PB 5/51
varieties.traits: Historical importance, moderate growth
varieties.growing_notes: One of early successful Malaysian clones, recommended for South India. Important in breeding history. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and South India

varieties.name: Tjir 1 (Tjirandji 1)
varieties.traits: Historical foundation clone, moderate growth, good wind resistance
varieties.growing_notes: Extremely important foundation clone. Cytoplasm donor for RRII 105, RRIM 600. One of earliest successful selections (1919-1926). Recommended for South India. Still planted for genetic diversity. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu nurseries

varieties.name: GT 1 (Gondang Tapen 1)
varieties.traits: Excellent wind resistance, vigorous growth, good survival rate, deep rooting for drought tolerance
varieties.growing_notes: One of most important foundation clones globally. Still widely planted in tropical monsoon climates, Cambodia, West Africa. Most stable clone for both growth and yield. Selected 1919-1926. Recommended for South India, Bangladesh, Meghalaya. Important parent in many breeding programs. Good overall resistance
varieties.availability: Widely available in tropical monsoon climates and Indian nurseries

varieties.name: PR 107
varieties.traits: Good rooting characteristics, moderate growth, used extensively as rootstock
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India. Important clone for rootstock use. Good drought resistance via deep rooting. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and South India

varieties.name: PR 255
varieties.traits: Good growth increment, vigorous, good stem circumference development
varieties.growing_notes: Successful early cross from Indonesia, widely tested. Recommended for Bangladesh multiclone plantings. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Indian nurseries

varieties.name: PR 261
varieties.traits: Good performance, moderate growth
varieties.growing_notes: One of successful early Indonesian crosses, mentioned in breeding literature Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available through import or specialty nurseries

varieties.name: PR 17
varieties.traits: Historical clone, moderate growth
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India, historical importance Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: BD 5 & BD 10
varieties.traits: Historical foundation clones, moderate vigor
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India. Part of early selection from 1919-1926. Historical importance in rubber breeding. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: AVROS 2037
varieties.origin: Indonesia, AVROS (Algemene Vereniging Rubber Planters Oostkust Sumatra), 1920s
varieties.traits: Good growth characteristics, part of important AVROS series
varieties.growing_notes: AVROS clones were important in Indonesian breeding. Used as parent in IAC 300 series development. Moderate resistance
varieties.availability: Available through specialty sources

varieties.name: IAC 15, IAC 35, IAC 40, IAC 41
varieties.traits: IAC 41 has 24% higher yield than RRIM 600. IAC 35 19% higher. Vigorous growth, good girth increment
varieties.growing_notes: High-yielding Brazilian clones showing excellent potential. Parents include RRIM 600, GT 711, PB 86, Tjir 1, PB 235. IAC 15 recommended for various regions. Good overall resistance
varieties.availability: Can be imported to India through ANRPC germplasm exchange

varieties.name: IAC 300 series (IAC 301, 302, 307, 311, 327, 329)
varieties.traits: Good vigor, thermal stability up to 300°C, excellent rubber quality
varieties.growing_notes: Parents from RRIM Malaysia and AVROS Indonesia selections. IAC 329 resistant to seasonal changes. Variable by clone
varieties.availability: Import through ANRPC germplasm programs

varieties.name: IAC 500 series (IAC 500-507, 511, 512)
varieties.traits: 40% of clones superior to RRIM 600. Vigorous growth, excellent rubber quality, thermal stability to 300°C
varieties.growing_notes: IAC 500, 501, 502, 503, 506 most promising. IAC 505, 507, 511, 512 recommended for fast growth. Very high yielding series. Good resistance
varieties.availability: Import through international programs

varieties.name: IAN 873
varieties.traits: Most stable clone for yield, good biocompatibility, excellent latex quality
varieties.growing_notes: Highly regarded for stability and latex quality. Greater bioactivity than RRIM 600. Good resistance
varieties.availability: Import through international germplasm exchange

varieties.name: IAN 2880, IAN 3156, IAN 3087, IAN 3193
varieties.traits: IAN 3156 and 3087 have good girth growth. Variable characteristics by clone
varieties.growing_notes: IAN 2880 and 3156 have establishment potential. Part of Amazonian genetic base expansion. Variable
varieties.availability: Import through germplasm programs

varieties.name: IRCA 18, IRCA 111, IRCA 130
varieties.traits: Active/rapid metabolism clones. IRCA 130 highest productivity. Good radial vegetative growth (2.9 cm/year). Well-balanced physiological profile
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for 5-10% of area for detailed evaluation. High-yielding modern clones. IRCA 111 parent in breeding programs. Low TPD sensitivity (4.9%), good disease tolerance
varieties.availability: Import through international programs

varieties.name: RRIC 100, RRIC 110, RRIC 121
varieties.traits: RRIC 121 outstanding growth and timber. RRIC 110 susceptible to Corynespora. RRIC 100 moderate performance
varieties.growing_notes: Sri Lankan clones very suitable for Tropical monsoon similar monsoon climate. RRIC 121 recommended for timber. Variable by clone
varieties.availability: Can be imported to India

varieties.name: RRIC 102
varieties.traits: Vigorous, very high bark thickness, satisfactory growth, slightly yellow latex
varieties.growing_notes: Group I recommendation in Sri Lanka. Good for higher elevations in tropical monsoon climates. Tolerant to Oidium leaf fall, recommended for high elevations
varieties.availability: Import from Sri Lanka

varieties.name: RRIC 52
varieties.traits: Moderate growth, good characteristics
varieties.growing_notes: Valuable for powdery mildew resistance breeding. Genome sequenced alongside PB 235 for disease resistance study. RESISTANT to powdery mildew (genome sequenced)
varieties.availability: Import from Sri Lanka

varieties.name: PCK-1, PCK-2
varieties.traits: Regional adaptations
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India Moderate
varieties.availability: Regional nurseries in tropical monsoon climates/Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: RRIM 118
varieties.traits: Good growth characteristics
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for South India Moderate
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: BPM 24
varieties.traits: Male sterile variety, unique mitochondrial genome
varieties.growing_notes: Important for breeding research due to cytoplasmic male sterility. Used in Sri Lankan trials. Variable
varieties.availability: Specialty breeding material

varieties.name: GI 1 (Glenshiel 1)
varieties.traits: Historical primary clone, moderate growth
varieties.growing_notes: Important foundation clone in Malaysian breeding. Part of early mother tree selections starting 1928. Moderate
varieties.availability: Rare, genetic conservation

varieties.name: 'Thadathil' (Fr. Thomas Selection)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates, India - discovered by Father Thomas in 1983 from RRII 105 saplings
varieties.traits: Exceptional growth to 57cm girth, visibly thicker bark, all propagated trees show identical uniform size and consistent latex production - 130 experimental trees with equal performance
varieties.yield_notes: DOUBLE normal yield - 1.75 liters latex/day per tree, 25 kg dry rubber vs normal production
varieties.growing_notes: REMARKABLE tropical monsoon climates discovery! One tree from 350 RRII-105 saplings showed exceptional performance. Published in 'Rubber Asia' and Rubber Board magazine. Scientific study ongoing to determine if genetic mutation or cultivation technique. Could be genetic breakthrough or result of specific fertilizer use. Represents potential major advancement for tropical monsoon climates rubber farming if genetics confirmed. Similar to RRII 105 parent, good overall resistance
varieties.availability: LIMITED - Father Thomas operates nursery in tropical monsoon climates with budwood from original tree. Farmers visiting to see trees. Patent application in process as of 2017
